source: walking-droplet / Faraday oil literature, 20 cSt silicone oil at 80 Hz
reference: Douady (1990); Kumar & Tuckerman, J. Fluid Mech. 279, 49 (1994); walking-droplet papers (Couder/Bush groups)
floor_reason: gamma_F/omega = 0.124 at 20 cSt puts the weak-damping (Benjamin-Ursell) approximation at the edge of its valid domain; accurate high-viscosity threshold needs the full-viscous Kumar-Tuckerman Floquet solver
independent_corroboration: this over-estimate independently confirms the known Phase-4 task: KT full-viscous version is required for high-viscosity accuracy (water is unaffected: kh>>1, low nu)
verdict: lambda GREEN (6.1%) / threshold = HONEST FLOOR (not passed, recorded)
detail: 20cSt oil 80Hz: lambda 5.04 vs lit 4.75mm; gamma_F 5.3 vs lit 4.2-4.3g (weak-damping marginal at 20cSt -> needs Kumar-Tuckerman full-viscous, already a Phase-4 task)
